description: Execute a Python script from inline content (scriptcontent) or an existing file (file). The two sources are mutually exclusive. Script content is written to a temporary file under .ide/tmp/scripts/, executed with the detected Python interpreter, and then deleted. Returns stdout and stderr. Supports pip dependency auto-install, CLI arguments, and async execution mode. The 'python' parameter allows specifying a custom interpreter path; otherwise, auto-detects from PATH.
name: execute_python_script

dir.description: Working directory for execution (absolute path). If empty, defaults to the project root.

scriptcontent.description: The complete Python script content to execute. Mutually exclusive with 'file'.

file.description: Path to an existing Python file to execute. Mutually exclusive with 'scriptcontent'.

args.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the script.

pip.description: List of pip packages to auto-install before running the script. Idempotent — skips already-installed packages.

async.description: If true, runs the script asynchronously and returns a task_id immediately. Use process_task_status/process_wait to check completion. Default: false.

python.description: Path to the Python interpreter. If empty, auto-detects (python then python3) from system PATH.
